title: Chaotic spin dynamics of elongated spinor condensates
authors: Jose Reyes-Calderón, Albert Gallemí, Carsten Klempt, Luis Santos
abstract: Elongated spin-$1$ condensates present a highly non-trivial local magnetization dynamics, due to the interplay between nonlinear and quantum effects stemming from the inhomogeneous density profile. This interplay results in different dynamical regimes after an initial global quench. In particular, we show that the system may display the coexistence of markedly different dynamical domains separated by a robust interface that acts as a spatial excited-state quantum phase transition. Furthermore, the local spinor dynamics may enter a chaotic regime characterized by irregular evolution and exponential sensitivity to initial conditions. We map the universal phase diagram distinguishing regular and chaotic regimes, which may be probed in on-going experiments.
summary: This theoretical study investigates the complex, non-equilibrium dynamics of elongated spin-1 spinor BECs. The authors demonstrate that the interplay of quantum and nonlinear effects can lead to the coexistence of distinct dynamical domains separated by spatial quantum phase transitions. Furthermore, they map out a universal phase diagram revealing a chaotic regime, offering new diagnostic tools for experimental quantum gas research.
main_problem: The paper investigates the complex, non-trivial local magnetization dynamics in elongated spin-1 spinor Bose-Einstein condensates (BECs). It focuses on the interplay between nonlinear and quantum effects that can lead to chaotic dynamics and spatial excited-state quantum phase transitions (ESQPTs).
main_result: The system can exhibit the coexistence of distinct dynamical domains separated by a robust ESQPT, and the local dynamics can enter a chaotic regime characterized by exponential sensitivity to initial conditions. This behavior is mapped onto a universal dynamical phase diagram.
method: The analysis involves solving coupled Gross-Pitaevskii equations (GPEs) in different regimes (SMA, LDR) and characterizing the dynamics using diagnostics like the power spectrum $P(\omega)$ and the divergence of perturbed trajectories.
model_or_system: The system is an elongated spin-1 BEC, described by an energy functional involving spin components and interactions. The dynamics are analyzed in regimes ranging from the Transversal-SMA approximation to the Local Density Regime (LDR).
key_observables: Local magnetization dynamics, the existence and nature of ESQPTs, and the divergence of trajectories (used to quantify chaos).
important_parameters: The degree of inhomogeneity ($\eta$), the spin-interaction parameters ($g, c$), and the initial quench parameters ($\xi_0, P_{00}$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes an elongated harmonic trap ($\omega_\perp \gg \omega$) and focuses on ferromagnetic BECs ($c < 0$). Approximations include the SMA and LDR limits.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate Bloch-sphere trajectories in different phases (P' and BA'), sketch the Thomas-Fermi profile showing ESQPT domains, and map out dynamical phase diagrams using metrics like $R$ to distinguish regular from chaotic regimes.
paper_structure: The paper develops the theory by first establishing the governing equations in various approximations (SMA, LDR), then analyzing the resulting dynamics, identifying the coexistence of distinct domains separated by ESQPTs, and finally mapping the universal phase diagram that distinguishes regular from chaotic evolution.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to AMO physics as it explores non-equilibrium dynamics and quantum chaos in ultracold atomic systems, providing experimental signatures (like domain walls and chaotic signatures) that can be probed in BEC experiments.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Typical Bloch-sphere trajectories in the P′ phase (a) and the BA′ phase (b). (c) Sketch of of the Thomas-Fermi profile with spatial dynamical BA′ and P′ domains and an ESQPT at x = ±xc. Figures (d) and (e) show the spin dynamics in the LDR for η = 103.9 and ξ0 = 0.45 in the absence and the presence of quantum torque, respectively. Note the destabilization of the domain wall. Figures (f) and (g) show the same for η = 103.9 and ξ0 = 0.58. There are no domains in the LDR, but the different time scale of the local dynamics results eventually in a large quantum torque that destabilizes the LDR evolution.

FIG. 2. Dynamics of | sin (φ(x, τ))| for P00 = 0.5, η = 4.82, and (a) ξ0 = 0.38 (a), ξ0 = 0.54 (b), and ξ0 = 1.01 (c). In Fig. (a) the yellow dashed lines indicate the ESQPT at x = ±xc. (d) Dynamical phase diagram as a function of ξ0 and P00, for η = 4.82. (e) Dynamical phase diagram as a function of ξ0 and η for P00 = 0.5. In plot (d) and (e), the color indicates the value of R (see text). Note the intricate structure of the chaotic regime. In plot (e), the solid yellow line marks the transition between the purely BA′ regime, and the coexistence regime. In both diagrams the dashed green lines indicate the transition between the regular coexistence regime and the irregular regime. We have...

FIG. 3. (a) For η = 4.82 and P00 = 0.5, we depict the aver- age distance d ¯P0(τ) (see text) between the spatially-averaged population ¯P0(τ) for an unperturbed case and a perturbed one (initially d ¯P0(τ = 0) = 10−4). For ξ0 = 0.49 (irreg- ular regime), the purple continuous line shows an exponen- tial growth of the distance with time up to its maximum. In contrast, for ξ0 = 0.25 (regular coexistence regime), the distance (dashed green line) remains very small. The cor- responding Bloch-Sphere representations for the spatially- averaged spin are depicted in (b) and (c).
